# Contributing

Thanks for your interest in contributing to a DRUNIK project. This guide applies across the [drunikbe](https://github.com/drunikbe) organization — individual repositories may add their own, more specific contributing notes in a `CONTRIBUTING.md` at the repo root.

## Before you start

- Read the project's `README.md` to understand what it does and how it's built.
- Skim open issues and discussions to check whether your idea is already being worked on.
- For anything non-trivial, open an issue or discussion first so we can align on the approach before you write code.

## Reporting bugs and requesting features

Use the templates in the relevant repository's **Issues** tab. See [SUPPORT.md](./SUPPORT.md) for guidance on what to include.

For security vulnerabilities, follow [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) instead of opening a public issue.

## Pull requests

1. Fork the repository and create a topic branch from `main`.
2. Make your change. Keep the diff focused — one logical change per PR is much easier to review than a grab-bag.
3. Add or update tests where they make sense for the project.
4. Update documentation if behaviour or interfaces change.
5. Open a pull request and fill in the template.

We'll do our best to review within a reasonable timeframe. Smaller, well-scoped PRs land faster.

## Commit messages

- Write in the imperative mood ("Add X", not "Added X" or "Adds X").
- Keep the subject under ~72 characters; put detail in the body.
- Explain *why* the change is being made, not just *what* it does — the diff already shows the what.

## Code style

Follow the conventions of the repository you're contributing to. If a project uses a formatter or linter, run it before opening the PR. If you're unsure, match the surrounding code.

## Licensing

Unless a repository specifies otherwise, contributions are accepted under the same licence as the project. By submitting a pull request, you confirm that you have the right to submit the work under that licence.

## Code of Merit

All contributors are expected to follow our [Code of Merit](./CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

## Getting help

If you're stuck, open a discussion in the relevant repository or reach out via the channels listed in [SUPPORT.md](./SUPPORT.md).
